findings.title: North Star Metric: Monthly Active Users
findings.description: The North Star metric for EnterpriseSuite is the Monthly Active Users (MAU), which reflects the engagement level of users across the platform. This metric is crucial as it indicates the overall health and adoption of the product.
assumptions: Increased MAU correlates with higher customer satisfaction and retention.
recommendations: Focus on strategies to increase MAU through enhanced features and user engagement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Churn Rate for Enterprise Customers
findings.description: The churn rate for enterprise customers is a primary KPI that measures the percentage of customers who discontinue their subscription over a given period. A lower churn rate indicates better customer retention.
assumptions: Churn rate directly impacts revenue and growth.
recommendations: Implement customer feedback loops to understand and reduce churn.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Uptime
findings.description: Uptime is a guardrail metric that measures the availability of the platform. Maintaining high uptime is critical to ensure customer satisfaction and trust.
assumptions: High uptime correlates with customer satisfaction.
recommendations: Monitor uptime closely and address any outages promptly.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: Customer Feedback on Features
findings.description: There is a gap in collecting structured feedback from customers regarding feature usage and satisfaction. This data is essential for understanding user needs and improving the product.
assumptions: Customer feedback is crucial for product development.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC before signing contracts.

recommendations: Implement a feedback collection mechanism such as surveys or interviews.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Churn Reduction Strategies
findings.description: Conduct experiments to test various churn reduction strategies, such as enhanced onboarding processes or customer engagement initiatives. Measure the impact on churn rate.
assumptions: Different strategies will have varying impacts on churn.
recommendations: Design A/B tests for different churn reduction strategies.

summary: The analysis identifies the Monthly Active Users as the North Star metric, with supporting KPIs including churn rate and uptime. Gaps in customer feedback collection and opportunities for experimentation in churn reduction strategies were also highlighted.
